primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign exchange exposure from intragroup dividend receivables can qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements under hedge accounting rules

primary_authority.reason: Establishes the requirement that hedged items must involve a party external to the reporting entity, with explicit statement that hedge accounting applies to intragroup transactions only in individual/separate statements, not consolidated statements
Contains the core eligibility criteria for hedged items and the specific exception for intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements

supporting_authority.reason: Defines when intragroup monetary items create foreign exchange exposure that is not fully eliminated on consolidation (different functional currencies between transacting entities)
Clarifies application of hedge accounting to net investment hedges and constraints on multiple overlapping hedging relationships in consolidated statements

peripheral_authority.reason: Specifies accounting mechanics for hedge types but does not address eligibility of intragroup items as hedged items
Addresses classification of hedge accounting gains/losses by category in profit or loss, not hedge eligibility

treatment_families.family: Hedging of intragroup monetary items under the specific exception
General exclusion of intragroup items from hedge accounting in consolidated statements
Hedge accounting applied in individual/separate financial statements

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for intragroup monetary item exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IAS 39 provide an explicit exception permitting foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items (including receivables) to qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements if the exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation per IAS 21

approaches.label: No hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Primary rule in IFRS 9 and IAS 39 prohibits designation of intragroup items as hedged items in consolidated financial statements; the general principle excludes all transactions between group entities from consolidated hedge accounting

approaches.label: Hedge accounting in separate or individual financial statements
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Both IFRS 9 and IAS 39 explicitly permit hedge accounting for intragroup transactions in the individual or separate financial statements of the entities involved, as the external party criterion is met at that level
